title: Ricinocarpus polymorphus
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus polymorphus is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. It is a member of the order Haloragales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Haloragaceae is a small group of aquatic plants, and Ricinocarpus polymorphus is one of the species within this family. Within the genus Ricinocarpus, it is a unique species due to its polymorphic nature. More research would be needed to classify it further.
uses: Ricinocarpus polymorphus, also known as the Australian saltbush, has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of the plant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and sore eyes. In traditional medicine, the plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties. intro: Ricinocarpus polymorphus, also known as the Desert Raisin or Carob Bush, is a small shrub or tree native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia.
